result:
All 26 call sites confirmed. Every one pairs correctly. Summary:

## os_malloc() callers in src/eap_common/ — audit result

**All 26 call sites correctly free their allocation on every exit path.** No leaks found.

| File | Line | Var | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| eap_eke_common.c | 350 | `id` | freed on prf error (359) and success (362) |
| eap_eke_common.c | 484 | `data` | freed on prf error (492) and success (501) |
| eap_eke_common.c | 525 | `data` | freed on prf error (540) and success (543) |
| eap_eke_common.c | 570 | `data` | freed on prf error (586) and success (589) |
| eap_ikev2_common.c | 31 | `nonces` | freed on prf error (39) and success (42) |
| eap_gpsk_common.c | 140 | `data` | freed on gkdf error (157) and success (160), before any later return |
| eap_gpsk_common.c | 298 | `seed` | single unconditional free (336) after switch, covers all `ret` outcomes |
| eap_gpsk_common.c | 371 | `data` | freed on gkdf error (389) and success (392) |
| eap_gpsk_common.c | 442 | `seed` | single unconditional free (469), covers both helper outcomes |
| eap_fast_common.c | 101 | `out` | freed on prf error (106); on success ownership transfers to caller (returned pointer) — correct, not a leak |
| eap_sake_common.c | 328 | `tmp` | single unconditional free (372), one exit path |
| eap_pwd_common.c | 159 | `prfbuf` | freed via common `fail:`/fallthrough cleanup block (281), covers all goto-fail and success paths |
| eap_pwd_common.c | 297 | `cruft` | freed on both hash-init errors (307, 324) and after last use on success (331), before the two later `return` statements — no use-after-free, no leak |
| eap_sim_common.c | 178 | `tmp` | single unconditional free (199) |
| eap_sim_common.c | 373 | `tmp` | single unconditional free (394) |
| eap_sim_common.c | 946 | `decrypted` | freed on both error paths (952, 962); ownership transfers to caller on success (returned) — correct |
| ikev2_common.c | 404 | `sign_data` | freed on prf error (420) and success (423) |
| ikev2_common.c | 496 | `decrypted` | freed on decrypt error (502) and padding error (510); ownership transfers to caller on success (returned) — correct |
| ikev2_common.c | 651 | `keybuf` | freed on prf_plus error (657) and unconditionally again at end (718) after all `keys->SK_*` derivations |
| ikev2_common.c | 663/671/679/687/695/703/711 | `keys->SK_d/SK_ai/SK_ar/SK_ei/SK_er/SK_pi/SK_pr` | not individually NULL-checked with early return; if any fails, `ikev2_keys_set()` (line 720) detects the missing pointer and `ikev2_free_keys()` (line 721) frees whichever of the 7 succeeded (`os_free(NULL)` is a no-op) — no leak, no double-free |

Notes on methodology: clangd's `compile_commands.json` only covers `eap_common.c`, `eap_peap_common.c`, and `chap.c` from this directory (verified via LSP `findReferences` on `os_malloc`'s definition at `src/utils/os_unix.c:677`, which returned zero hits in the other 8 eap_common files). Those 8 files aren't part of the indexed translation-unit set, so I manually traced every `os_malloc`/`os_free` pair via direct file reads rather than relying on LSP references for them. `grep -n "os_malloc(" src/eap_common/*.c` confirmed 26 total call sites, matching what was reviewed above; no calls exist in the directory's header files.
